name: Azure Key Vault
what_it_does: Azure managed key vault for secrets, encryption keys, and certificate lifecycle management with HSM-backed hardware security and Azure AD RBAC integration.
use_cases: Retrieving API secrets and connection strings for Azure-deployed agents via Managed Identity
Managing encryption keys for agent-processed sensitive data with BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
Automatic TLS certificate renewal and rotation for agent service endpoints
Storing and rotating database passwords with Key Vault references in App Service/AKS
Audit logging all secret access with Azure Monitor and Diagnostic Settings for compliance
not_for: Non-Azure deployments (use Vault or Secrets Manager for multi-cloud)
High-frequency secret reads without caching — latency adds up at 10K+ reads/day
Teams unfamiliar with Azure AD RBAC — access configuration can be complex
best_when: Your agents run in Azure and need production-grade secrets management with HSM-backed keys, certificate management, and deep Azure AD integration.
avoid_when: You're on AWS or GCP, need cross-cloud secrets management, or find Azure RBAC complexity prohibitive.

auth.notes: Azure AD with Managed Identity (recommended) or service principal. RBAC roles: Key Vault Secrets User (read), Key Vault Secrets Officer (write). Access policies (legacy) or RBAC (recommended).

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; very low cost
pricing.paid_tiers: Standard: $0.03/10K operations for secrets; HSM-protected keys: $1/key/month + $0.03/10K ops; Premium: hardware security
pricing.notes: Extremely cost-efficient — most agents spend <$5/month. HSM-backed keys are significantly more expensive but required for compliance.

performance.rate_limits: 2000 transactions/10s for secrets; 400 TPS for keys

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Azure SDK errors have ErrorCode property; 403 can mean either missing role or soft-deleted vault
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SetSecret with same name creates new version; all operations are effectively idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: Two permission models: Access Policies (legacy) vs RBAC — choose RBAC for new vaults, they don't mix well
Soft-delete is enabled by default and CANNOT be disabled — deleted secrets enter 7-90 day recoverable state
Vault endpoint uses vault-specific URL (https://myvault.vault.azure.net) not a regional endpoint — hardcode carefully
Key Vault references in App Service/AKS use @Microsoft.KeyVault(VaultName=...) syntax — URI format not URL
Secret versions are immutable — update creates a new version; disable old versions to prevent rotation issues

security_score_components.security_notes: HSM-backed keys with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 for Premium tier. Managed Identity eliminates credential management. Private endpoints for VNet isolation.
